task_completion.description: Load the populated places shapefile into gvSIG Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG Desktop application is open, and 'ne_110m_populated_places' is visible in the Table of Contents (left panel) or its attribute table is open.

task_completion.description: Add a new field named 'URBAN_CAT' to the attribute table
task_completion.visual_evidence: The attribute table shows a new column header named 'URBAN_CAT', or a Field Manager/Table Edit dialog shows the creation of this field (String, length 20).

In the attribute table, rows where the 'POP_MAX' column has a value of 10000000 or greater show 'Megacity' in the 'URBAN_CAT' column. This might be seen during a Field Calculator operation or in the final table.
In the attribute table, rows where the 'POP_MAX' column has a value less than 10000000 show 'City' in the 'URBAN_CAT' column.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the shapefile
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'Finish editing' and accepts saving the changes, clicks the save button while editing, or a dialog confirming the save operation is visible.

integrity.description: The agent used gvSIG Desktop to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: All GIS operations and attribute table edits should occur within the gvSIG Desktop graphical user interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use an external spreadsheet program to edit the DBF
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots showing the .dbf file being edited in LibreOffice Calc, Excel, or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not use Python scripts (e.g., geopandas) in the terminal to modify the shapefile directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The shapefile is located at /home/ga/gvsig_data/cities/ne_110m_populated_places.shp. The attribute table contains 243 records. Exactly 17 cities have a POP_MAX >= 10,000,000 and should be classified as 'Megacity'. The remaining 226 cities should be classified as 'City'. The new field must be exactly named 'URBAN_CAT'.
